title: ajuga nipponensis
intro: Ajuga nipponensis, also known as bugleweed, is a type of flowering plant native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an antispasmodic and astringent, and its potential to lower thyroid hormone levels. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and stress, and has been used to treat conditions like hyperthyroidism and insomnia. Ajuga nipponensis is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also dried and used in capsule or powder form.
